title: 2026 State of the Science Summit on Reducing Methane from Animal Agriculture
ai_summary: Three-day summit at UC Davis (June 16-18, 2026) convening researchers, producers, and policymakers on livestock methane reduction — feed additives, genetics, MRV. Core relevance to LOCOL's Thai beef + methane stack; the single best annual gathering in this space. Register by June 5; virtual option available at $75 if travel is hard.

details.organizer: Spark Climate Solutions + UC Davis CLEAR Center + CDFA

title: Registration · 2026 SOS Summit (UC Davis) — same event as above
ai_summary: ⚠️ DUPLICATE of Spark Climate item above — this is the actual registration page. $400 in-person / $75 virtual / $50 student. Merge with the other SOS Summit entry; keep this one as the action link.

title: Carbon Pulse article 503102 (paywalled — manual review needed)
ai_summary: fetch failed (403 paywall) — review manually. Carbon Pulse is a carbon-markets trade publication; article likely covers methane / agriculture carbon credits. Worth a quick scan if a subscriber is on the team.

details.takeaway: Paywalled carbon-market news — manual review needed

title: 400/1600 Danish farms report Bovaer health issues + 3 methane contract clauses
ai_summary: 🚩 RISK SIGNAL — Bullvine analysis tying the Danish Bovaer health-issue reports (~25% of 1,600 farms) to the EFSA 2026 review and three contract clauses buyers should renegotiate. Directly relevant to LOCOL's offtake strategy — read alongside the $73/cow gap piece before signing any 3-NOP-based methane contract.

details.takeaway: Bovaer offtake contracts need defensive clauses given EFSA review + Danish farm reports

title: The $73-a-cow gap in your 2027 Bovaer contract
ai_summary: 🚩 RISK SIGNAL — Bovaer costs $93-105/cow/year but a $0.12/cwt premium only yields ~$33, leaving a $40-73/cow gap producers must close via carbon credits or absorb. April 2026 Science paper shows 3-NOP plateaus at 28-30% because it only targets downstream methanogens. Critical input to LOCOL's Bovaer economics.

details.takeaway: 3-NOP plateaus at 28-30%; unit economics need carbon-credit stacking or alternatives

title: Cargill BeefUp Sustainability program
ai_summary: Cargill's umbrella program to cut GHG intensity of N. American beef — grazing, enteric methane R&D ($1M invested), food waste. Partners with McDonald's, Nestlé, Walmart, WWF, TNC. Potential channel for LOCOL if cocoa-fed Thai beef methane data is competitive; Cargill APAC is the right door. Long-shot lead — start by reading their methane RFPs.

details.key_terms: Sustainable beef sourcing / methane innovation partnerships

title: Public acceptance of methane-reducing feed additives (J. Dairy Sci, Apr 2026)
ai_summary: European 4-country survey (n=3,220) finds consumers prefer natural feed additives (seaweed, plant oils) over chemical 3-NOP, and that info-disclosure cuts acceptance of natural products but raises it for chemicals. Useful narrative input for LOCOL's cocoa-fed positioning vs Bovaer — natural-derived methane story may travel better with retail buyers and importers.

details.takeaway: Consumers favor natural methane-reducing additives over chemical 3-NOP — supports LOCOL's cocoa-feed narrative

title: CARB public solicitation on dairy/livestock methane regulations (deadline May 29)
ai_summary: CARB extended public comment deadline to May 29, 2026 on potential methane regs for CA dairy/livestock under SB1383. 30 questions across data, mitigation, regulatory design. LOCOL is not a CA stakeholder so 'act' is unlikely, but precedent matters — California regulation drives global supply chain expectations buyers will bring to Thailand.

details.takeaway: CA methane reg design will shape global buyer-side expectations within 2-3 years

title: UN approves first Article 6.4 Paris Agreement credit issuance
ai_summary: Feb 26, 2026: Article 6.4 Supervisory Body issued first Paris-mechanism credits to a Myanmar clean-cooking project, with Korea as authorizing partner. 165+ CDM-transition projects in queue including agriculture. Relevant baseline for LOCOL if methane-mitigation work in Thailand is ever monetized via Article 6 (Thailand has begun bilateral cooperation).

details.takeaway: Article 6.4 now operational — Thai methane projects could plausibly become host-authorized credits

title: EFSA reviewing Bovaer data after Danish farm reports
ai_summary: 🚩 RISK SIGNAL — EFSA opened a public data call (deadline Mar 31, 2026, now CLOSED) after ~25% of 1,600 Danish farms reported digestive/metabolic issues since Dec 2025 mandatory Bovaer use. Outcome could affect EU authorization. Key risk signal for any LOCOL methane plan that leans on 3-NOP — diversify into seaweed/lipid/genetics in parallel.

details.takeaway: EFSA reviewing Bovaer safety — material risk to any 3-NOP-dependent methane plan

title: JBS USA breaks ground on $150M Cactus beef plant expansion
ai_summary: JBS investing $150M expanding its Cactus, TX beef plant (3,600 employees, $3.3B/yr in local cattle), completion early 2027. Pure industry news — bullish signal on US beef demand despite small herd. Not directly actionable for LOCOL but watch JBS sustainability commitments as a buyer-side reference point.

details.takeaway: JBS doubling down on US beef capacity — buyer-side demand signal

title: EPA Global Methane Initiative (Thailand is a partner country)
ai_summary: EPA-led 48-country partnership on methane recovery & use (coal mine, landfill, organic waste). Agriculture is not a primary focus. Thailand is a GMI partner country — possible technical-assistance / co-funding angle for manure or organic-waste methane work, but enteric methane is outside scope.

details.takeaway: Thailand is a GMI partner — possible TA channel for waste-side methane, not enteric

title: CH4 Global wins 2 SEAL sustainability awards for Methane Tamer
ai_summary: CH4 Global won SEAL Environmental Initiative + Sustainable Product awards (Feb 2026) for its Asparagopsis-based Methane Tamer (up to 90% reduction at 0.5% of feed) and its Louth Bay EcoPark (80 tonnes/yr). Methane-reduced beef already retailing in South Australia. Direct benchmark + potential APAC supplier for LOCOL via their Mitsubishi partnership.

details.takeaway: CH4 Global is the APAC commercial leader on seaweed methane reduction — both reference and possible supplier

title: F&B Feb 2026 review: cultivated cacao, fertilizer trade, beef prices
ai_summary: Monthly F&B roundup: Barry Callebaut & Puratos launching cultivated cocoa; EU-Russia fertilizer collapse threatens 2022-style price shock; US beef prices rising 6x food inflation (smallest herd in 75 years); dsm-firmenich divested Animal Nutrition (~€2.2B) — relevant for Bovaer future ownership. Three direct touch-points for LOCOL: cocoa cost basis, fertilizer for cocoa, and US beef demand signal.

details.takeaway: dsm-firmenich exited Animal Nutrition — Bovaer ownership change ahead, watch buyer

title: Verra to launch Scope 3 Standard (S3S) in Q3 2026
ai_summary: Verra's Scope 3 Standard v1.0 Phase 1 launches Q3 2026 — introduces Intervention Units (IUs) and S3IUs in a public registry, third-party audited, aligned with GHGP/SBTi. Material for LOCOL if downstream buyers (food brands) want to fund methane interventions in our supply chain as Scope 3 reductions. Worth aligning early on MRV format.

details.takeaway: Verra S3S could be the rails for LOCOL methane interventions to count as buyer Scope 3

title: Vet: Bovaer affected every Danish herd that used it
ai_summary: 🚩 RISK SIGNAL — Danish vet Dr. Anders Ring (580 cows) says he knows no farm without Bovaer problems — digital dermatitis, diarrhea, mastitis, milk-fever, milk-production drops. Symptoms reversed in 2 days after stopping. SEGES survey: 376/551 farms reported reduced milk yield. Mandatory use rule (Oct 2025, 50+ cow herds, 80 days/yr) likely to be revisited. Strong evidence base against any 3-NOP-only methane plan.

details.takeaway: Field evidence supports EFSA concern — keep 3-NOP optional, not core, in LOCOL stack

title: EPA methane regulations 2026: what farm operators need to know
ai_summary: Compliance-firm explainer on US move from voluntary to mandatory ag methane management — Waste Emissions Charge rising to $1,500/tonne by 2026, CA dairies 1,000+ head mandatory by 2028, NY in 2-3 years. Affects CAFOs, dairy, swine, poultry. Useful as a forecast of where buyers will eventually push Thai suppliers, but no direct LOCOL action today.

details.takeaway: Buyer-side regulatory pressure on methane mounting in US — Thailand will feel it via contracts

title: Organic Valley + Stonyfield + UNFI + Whole Foods launch VIUs insetting
ai_summary: Mar 3, 2026: four-way value-chain partnership creates Verified Impact Units (VIUs) on SustainCERT VIVID — farm-level methane/solar/manure interventions financed by retail+distributor co-investment instead of offsets. Template LOCOL could pitch to Thai/APAC dairy retailers for cocoa-fed beef methane interventions. Worth a playbook write-up.

details.takeaway: Insetting playbook template — co-funded farm interventions verified on SustainCERT VIVID
details.related_companies: Organic Valley, Stonyfield, UNFI, Whole Foods, SustainCERT

title: Verra VM0042 v3.0 ALM methodology revision (consultation closed)
ai_summary: ⚠️ Consultation CLOSED Mar 31, 2026 — now in Independent Expert Review. VM0042 v3.0 covers soil carbon for improved ag land management. Focus is SOC not enteric methane, so peripheral to LOCOL. Track for grazing-system soil carbon stacking if LOCOL ever wants to bundle soil credits with methane reduction story.

details.takeaway: Soil carbon methodology — peripheral, but useful for grazing co-benefit stacking

title: Greener Cattle Initiative — enteric methane research fund (FFAR)
ai_summary: FFAR-managed consortium (ADM, Elanco, Genus, Nestlé, JBS, etc.) funds up to $4.76M for 3-yr enteric methane research — nutrition, genetics, microbiome, MRV. Original 2022 RFP closed; check for current/upcoming rounds. LOCOL fit only via an academic co-PI partner (e.g., Thai university) — not direct. Worth a fit-check call to FFAR.

sponsor.org_name: Foundation for Food and Agriculture Research (FFAR)

title: Marble Founder-in-Residence — Enteric Methane (Paris)
ai_summary: Marble (Paris climate-tech venture builder) offers €2,500/mo stipend + €250k pre-seed for founders building rumen-microbiome enteric-methane startups; founders keep 80% equity, France visa. Rolling cohort. Misaligned with LOCOL (we're operating, not pre-incorporation), but useful contact for Thai-side scientific talent recruitment.

details.eligibility: PhD-level rumen microbiome / animal nutrition + entrepreneurial intent

title: Danish farmers raise concerns about Bovaer (Nov 2025)
ai_summary: 🚩 RISK SIGNAL — Nov 2025 (earliest of the Danish Bovaer cluster): farmers report fever, diarrhea, fertility loss, collapsed cows, digital dermatitis after Oct 1 2025 mandatory rule (50+ cow herds, 80 days/yr). ~1,400 of 2,000 farms using it. Context for the EFSA review and later articles.

details.takeaway: First wave of Danish Bovaer health-issue reporting — context for the EFSA review

title: RenewCred raises ₹4.25 Cr seed for MRV-tech carbon credits
ai_summary: Jan 2026: Bengaluru's RenewCred raised ₹4.25 Cr seed (Campus Angels Network lead + Kairos, build3, ACT) for IoT+ML+blockchain MRV for high-integrity carbon credits — incl. methane reduction, biochar, EVs. Global South focus. Potential MRV vendor candidate for LOCOL if we want pan-Asian carbon infrastructure that isn't Verra-only.

details.takeaway: Indian MRV-tech startup; potential vendor for Asian carbon infrastructure

title: ArkeaBio $26.5M Series A (cattle methane vaccine, June 2024)
ai_summary: ⚠️ OLD NEWS (~2 years old) but still strategically relevant. ArkeaBio is a Boston biotech developing a livestock methane vaccine; June 2024 Series A of $26.5M led by Breakthrough Energy Ventures with AgriZeroNZ, The Grantham Foundation. Direct competitor/complement to Bovaer and Asparagopsis — a vaccine route would be radically simpler logistically than daily feed dosing, very relevant to LOCOL APAC operating model. Check their 2026 progress.

details.takeaway: Methane vaccine route — if it works, beats daily feed dosing on logistics

title: CH4 Global × Mitsubishi APAC partnership for Methane Tamer
ai_summary: ⚠️ 16-month-old news but HIGHLY strategic for LOCOL — Jan 2025: CH4 Global + Mitsubishi Corp partner to scale Methane Tamer (Asparagopsis, up to 90% reduction) in APAC — Australia commercial expansion, Japan validation (~4M cattle), and 'Southeast Asia' production exploration. Thailand not named but is the obvious candidate for SEA Asparagopsis cultivation. Direct competitor + possible LOCOL partnership target — explore co-cultivation or supply deal with Mitsubishi Thailand.

details.takeaway: Mitsubishi is scouting SEA for Asparagopsis cultivation — Thailand is in play

title: Australia DCCEEW livestock emissions reduction methodologies
ai_summary: Australian govt page covering livestock methods under ACCU scheme (beef-cattle herd management, feed quality, etc.). Useful methodology reference for designing a Thai analogue under Article 6 or voluntary registries. Reference material, not news.

details.takeaway: Australian ACCU livestock methods — methodology template for Thai context

title: Symbrosia $5.8M Series A-1 for SeaGraze (Asparagopsis)
ai_summary: Dec 2025: Hawaii's Symbrosia raised $5.8M Series A-1 (Idemitsu, One Small Planet) for SeaGraze Asparagopsis supplement — currently 2,500 head, scaling to 22,000 by Apr 2026, future 15-acre facility for 1.4M head. 700k head under offtake. FDA approval expected mid-2026. Direct CH4 Global competitor; option to import to Thailand if local cultivation lags.

details.takeaway: Symbrosia + CH4 Global both racing for Asparagopsis scale — supply may be import-competitive for Thailand

title: Practicality of delivering seaweed additive to grazing cattle (study)
ai_summary: Beef Magazine piece on a study testing how to actually get Asparagopsis into grazing (not feedlot) cattle — a critical question because Thai beef is largely smallholder/grazing, where daily dosing breaks down. Read for delivery-mechanism ideas (mineral lick, slow-release bolus).

details.takeaway: Grazing-delivery is the unsolved problem for seaweed additives — directly affects Thai applicability

title: Climate Curve $200k methane-mitigation prize
ai_summary: Climate Curve launched a $200,000 prize for methane mitigation innovations. Direct LOCOL fit (cocoa-fed methane reduction). Pull deadline and eligibility from the source URL or Climate Curve site, then fit-check this week. ⚠️ Need to verify deadline.

details.eligibility: Methane mitigation innovators (verify on source site)

title: UC Davis Feed Summit (verify vs SOS Summit duplicate)
ai_summary: ⚠️ POSSIBLE DUPLICATE of State of the Science Summit (also UC Davis, June 16-18 2026). Verify whether this is the same event or a separate feed-additive-focused track before registering twice.

title: CARB SB1383 information solicitation (PDF, Mar 2026)
ai_summary: Official CARB PDF (Mar 4, 2026) soliciting input on SB1383 methane regulations for CA dairy/livestock. Comment deadline extended to May 29, 2026 (in 4 days). LOCOL has no CA standing to file. Useful as primary source on CARB's question set — informs how Thai operations will eventually be benchmarked by US buyers.

details.takeaway: Primary CARB document — benchmark for how methane MRV will be asked of suppliers

title: USDA actions on state nutrition programs (off-scope)
ai_summary: ⚠️ OFF-SCOPE — USDA press release on nutrition program (SNAP/WIC-adjacent) administration changes. Not directly related to methane, beef, cocoa, or Thailand. Filed and archived.

details.takeaway: USDA nutrition admin update — off-scope for LOCOL

title: StoneX: cocoa market faces life after crisis
ai_summary: StoneX market analysis on post-crisis cocoa pricing/supply. Critical for LOCOL cocoa-fed beef unit economics — cocoa input cost is a core variable. Pull the headline numbers (forward curve, West Africa output) manually and update the cocoa cost model.

details.takeaway: Cocoa price normalization affects LOCOL cocoa-feed cost basis directly

title: Regenagri Insetting Standard 2.0 launched
ai_summary: Apr 7, 2026: Regenagri released Insetting Standard 2.0 — new Innovative Regenerative Practice (IRP) additionality test, flexible methodology choice, enhanced MRV, updated SOC quantification. 25 projects covering 361k ha. Livestock/methane not the headline focus but SOC stacking applies to grazing systems. Plausible insetting standard for LOCOL cocoa-fed beef if Verra-S3S is too heavy.

details.takeaway: Lighter insetting standard option vs Verra S3S — viable rails for early LOCOL claims

title: Bovaer product page (dsm-firmenich)
ai_summary: Vendor page: Bovaer claims 30% dairy / 45% beef methane reduction, approved in EU, UK, Japan, Canada + 70 countries, used in 500k+ cows. Thailand status not listed — likely no local registration. Note dsm-firmenich is divesting Animal Nutrition (~€2.2B), so Bovaer ownership will change. If LOCOL ever uses Bovaer, contact info.bovaer@dsm-firmenich.com and check Thai FDA/DLD registration first.

details.takeaway: Bovaer not registered in Thailand; ownership about to change — wait before any commitment

title: Fermtech £2.5M for koji-fermented cocoa-shell ingredient
ai_summary: Apr 8, 2026: Oxford's Fermtech raised £2.5M (Elbow Beach lead) for koji-fermentation of cocoa shells into a 25-33% cheaper cocoa-powder replacement with up to 98% lower CO2. Adjacent to LOCOL — if cocoa-shell upcycling spreads, raw cocoa-shell supply (a candidate cattle feed input) may tighten. Watch for supply-side competition.

details.takeaway: Cocoa-shell upcycling startups may compete for the same waste-stream LOCOL uses as feed

title: Triple Bio €1.5M for lipid-tech (RumeNRG) boosting milk + cutting methane
ai_summary: 🆕 FRESH (5 days ago) — May 20, 2026: Netherlands-based Triple Bio emerged from stealth with €1.5M (Nucleus, Positron, Climate Club) for RumeNRG — lipid encapsulation that cuts methane-inhibitor dose 500-fold AND redirects hydrogen for 5-10% milk yield gains. Founded by ex-Deep Branch + ex-Mootral team. Net-positive economics could solve the $73/cow gap. Watch as a potential post-Bovaer technology for LOCOL.

details.takeaway: Lipid encapsulation + hydrogen redirection could give net-positive methane economics
details.related_companies: Triple Bio, Nucleus, Positron, Climate Club

title: ReFED $2M Catalytic Grant Fund to 9 methane-focused orgs
ai_summary: 🆕 FRESH (5 days ago) — May 20, 2026: ReFED awarded $2M across 9 grantees (Black Earth Compost, CREF, Fermeate, Gilly, Golden State Foods, NewTerra, Organicycle, Rust Belt Riders, U of Georgia) on food-waste methane + beef/dairy supply-chain methane. Since 2022, 21 grantees attracted $42.6M follow-on. Watch grantfund.refed.org for next round — US 501(c)(3) status likely required so LOCOL would need a US fiscal sponsor.

details.takeaway: ReFED is an active methane funder; next round = potential LOCOL apply with US fiscal sponsor

title: CDRF Dairy Plus Program (Climate-Smart Commodities, CA)
ai_summary: CDRF/CDFA Dairy Plus Program funds advanced manure management on CA dairies — $43.6M across 37 projects so far (87% of a $75M USDA grant). 2026 application window 'planned early 2026' — register for notifications. CA-only and dairy-only, so LOCOL is not directly eligible. File as a template for any future Thai equivalent program design.
